articles.title: Spirit's exit lifts airfares, but budget model remains under pressure
articles.excerpt: The collapse of Spirit Airlines, one of the industry's fiercest discounters, has given U.S. budget carriers more room to lift fares, but its exit does little to fix ​the long-running strain on the cheap-flight model.

articles.title: After 1973 Oil Shock, Stock Market Didn't Recover For 20 Years. Can that Happen Again?
articles.excerpt: After 1973's oil shock, the Dow crashed 40% and plunged into what was later dubbed a “lost decade.” Adjusted for inflation, it took the index roughly 20 years to recover.

articles.title: Swatch shareholder vote tests Hayek grip after advisers back Wood
articles.excerpt: Swatch is bracing for a showdown at its annual meeting on Tuesday ​after proxy advisers came out in support of investor Steven Wood, challenging the Hayek family's control as the watchmaker underperforms ‌rivals.
